evidence.scientific_clarity: Line+shading plot with log-scale y-axis clearly shows Ours converging faster with lower final loss; double-headed arrow annotation at step 49 highlights the gap; three methods are visually distinguishable.
evidence.typography: PDF uses Type3 (bitmap) fonts — /Subtype /Type3 for both DejaVuSans and DejaVuSans-Oblique — instead of Type42/TrueType; script lacks pdf.fonttype=42 setting; DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default.
evidence.palette: Gray/blue/red palette is colorblind-accessible (distinguishable in deuteranopia); red is reserved for 'Ours' (directional positive), blue and gray for baselines — reasonable directional discipline, but red/green convention not violated.
evidence.layout_density: 5.5x3.8 inch figure; tight_layout used; annotation text at final step fits within xlim padding; legend placed upper-right and does not overlap data; no excessive whitespace observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and includes specific numerical claim (Delta=0.155); title in figure duplicates caption info (redundant); legend is inside the plot which is acceptable; no obvious issues that would trigger a Reviewer 2 complaint beyond the font type.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains Type3 bitmap-embedded fonts (not selectable/editable text); script does not set matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42 or plt.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is rasterised in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial (PDF 21 kB, PNG 80 kB, SVG 45 kB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states method, n=5 seeds, 95% CI shading, and gives a specific quantitative finding: Ours is Delta=0.155 below next-best Method-A at step 49, plus notes tighter CIs throughout.

overall_strengths: Strong scientific communication: log-scale axes, CI bands, and annotated gap at final step make the key claim immediately readable.
Caption is complete and quantitative, citing seed count, CI definition, and a precise numerical finding.
overall_weaknesses: PDF fonts are Type3 (bitmapped), not Type42/TrueType — text is not selectable or editable; script is missing `matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] = 42`.
Default DejaVu Sans typeface is used throughout; no explicit font choice (e.g. Helvetica, Arial, or a publication-standard sans-serif) and no fonttype=42 setting anywhere in the generation script.
